content_warning_text: This scenario shows a real-feeling online conversation that contains grooming tactics — manipulation techniques adults sometimes use to target young people. The content is educational and never explicit. You will read the conversation and identify the warning signs.
title: The Too-Perfect Stranger
introduction: Read the conversation below between Jamie (14) and Alex, someone they met in a gaming forum. Tap any message you think contains a warning sign. When you're done, press Submit to see how you did.

script.text: Hey! I saw your comment about Hollow Knight in the forum and honestly your take was so good?? Nobody else in that thread actually gets the lore the way you do.
oh thanks haha I've been playing for like 2 years
That's actually impressive. Most people give up way before that.
Two years! You clearly know way more than me. How old are you if you don't mind me asking? You write so maturely.
script.explanation: Asking someone's age early in a conversation, combined with a compliment about how 'mature' they seem, is a common opening tactic. It establishes how young the target is while making them feel flattered.

script.text: 14?? Wow you're so mature for your age. I'm 19 but honestly I feel like I connect with you better than most people my age. You just... get it, you know?
script.explanation: 'You're so mature for your age' is one of the most recognised grooming phrases. It's designed to make the target feel special and different — and to normalise a relationship with someone significantly older.

script.text: haha yeah I guess. I like talking to people who actually know the game
Honestly the lore in this game is incredible. The developers put so much into it.
Same. Honestly your friends probably don't appreciate how smart you are. Most people your age are so immature. You're different.
script.explanation: Suggesting that a target's friends 'don't appreciate' them is an isolation tactic. The goal is to make Jamie feel that Alex is the only one who truly understands them — increasing emotional dependence.

script.text: yeah sometimes lol. they're into different stuff
I feel like we have such a real connection. I've never met anyone like you online. Can I add you on Instagram? I'd love to keep talking outside the forum.
script.explanation: Moving the conversation off a public forum to a private or less moderated platform is a deliberate step. It removes visibility and isolates the target further.

script.text: Cool! I'll send you a request. My username is the same on there as here.
Amazing. Hey — let's keep this between us yeah? My friends wouldn't get it, and your parents might not understand that we're just friends. It's easier.
script.explanation: This is one of the clearest warning signs in any interaction. Healthy friendships don't need to be secret. Asking someone to hide a relationship from their parents is a major red flag — full stop.

script.text: You're really special Jamie. I think about our conversations a lot. You make me feel understood in a way nobody else does. 💙
script.explanation: Creating a sense of deep emotional bond very quickly — especially across an age gap — is a manipulation technique. It makes the target feel responsible for the other person's feelings.

learning_objectives: Recognise that 'you're so mature for your age' is a warning phrase, not a compliment
Identify secrecy requests as one of the clearest grooming red flags
Understand that moving to private platforms is a deliberate tactic
See how isolation from friends is used to increase dependence
Know that grooming feels warm and flattering — that's what makes it dangerous
debrief:
This conversation shows the opening stages of online grooming — before anything explicitly harmful happens. That's intentional. By the time things escalate, the manipulator has already built trust and dependence. Recognising these **early** signs is the most important skill.

**What made this dangerous:**
Every message from Alex felt friendly. The flattery felt good. The 'special connection' felt real. That's exactly how grooming works — it doesn't feel threatening at first.

**The clearest red flags were:**
- Asking Jamie's age early, then calling them 'mature'
- Suggesting Jamie's friends don't appreciate them
- Asking to move to a private platform
- Requesting secrecy from parents
- Creating a sense of deep emotional bond very quickly

**What Jamie should do:**
Stop replying. Block Alex. Tell a trusted adult — a parent, guardian, or teacher — straight away. Jamie has done nothing wrong.

**You are never at fault.** Groomers are skilled and patient. Knowing these patterns is how you protect yourself.
